signals.reasoning: Keep managing the live MON short. It is now retaining about 84% of peak P&L, has no active warning pressure, and is behaving like a real winner rather than a giveback risk.
BTC has weakened, but veto keeps even decent directional ideas in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH is also too mixed to justify action while the event brake is active.
SOL still has constructive structure, but veto should keep it out of the buy bucket for now.
STBL remains a clean bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks vulnerable to reversal.
ZEREBRO lacks enough clean edge or trade quality to justify action here.
MAVIA is still a valid fade because funding is positive, crowding is high, and the market quality is too weak to trust on the long side.
HEMI remains a bearish fade candidate because funding is strongly positive and the structure is soft enough that upside continuation looks fragile.
DASH remains one of the best long candidates on pure mechanics, but veto should keep it in watch mode rather than as a fresh buy.
HMSTR is too illiquid and noisy to be actionable.
CHILLGUY remains extended and crowded, but not clean enough to trade under veto conditions.
